title: Sien Sovv
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Youmay|the Sullustan Supreme Commander during the Yuuzhan Vong War|[[Sien|the Sullustan]] during the [[First Jedi Purge]]}} {{Character |type=GFFA |image= |name=Sien Sovv |homeworld=[[Sullust/Legends|Sullust]] |birth=[[40 BBY/Legends|40 BBY]], Sullust |death=[[36 ABY]], over [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] |species=[[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustan]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height=Approx. 1.5 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes=[[Brown]] |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] *[[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|The same Sovv who cost you Coruscant?''"
"''His reputation is undeserved, as he has recently proven. And even if it was, we need someone like him to lead us to the right kind of victory: only someone who has faced losing everything can sympathize with a defeated enemy.|Gilad Pellaeon and Luke Skywalker|''[[The New Jedi Order: Force Heretic I: Remnant]]''}} '''Sien Sovv''' was the [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustan]] [[Supreme Commander (Alliance)/Legends|Supreme Commander]] of the [[New Republic Defense Force/Legends|Combined Defense Forces of the New Republic]] and later the [[Galactic Alliance Defense Force]] from roughly [[23 ABY/Legends|23 ABY]] to [[29 ABY/Legends|29 ABY]], commanding the Defense Force just prior to and throughout the whole [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], and again from [[29 ABY/Legends|29 ABY]] to [[36 ABY]]. Partially responsible for the disastrous loss of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] to the Yuuzhan Vong armada, [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] Sovv nevertheless retained the confidence of the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and Galactic Alliance leadership and was allowed to remain on his post. A fleet under his command [[Battle of Ebaq|narrowly defeated]] the Yuuzhan Vong at [[Ebaq 9]], an event that marked the turning point of the war, and he regained some of his former prestige. Sovv also served on [[Cal Omas]]' first [[High Council (Galactic Alliance)|High Council]] from [[27 ABY/Legends|27 ABY]] to [[29 ABY/Legends|29 ABY]]. ==Biography== [[File:First_Fleet_advance.jpg|thumb|left|Sien Sovv's [[Delvardus campaign|campaign]] against the [[Eriadu Authority]]]] ===Early years=== Sien Sovv was born on [[Sullust/Legends|Sullust]], where he started a family. Like many [[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustans]] he had a large family, with over seven wives and numerous children. He eventually joined the [[New Republic Defense Force/Legends|New Republic Defense Force]], where he slowly rose though the ranks. He developed a reputation as a good organizer, though he was never noted for possessing any special degree of tactical or strategic prowess. When [[Sander Delvardus]] of the [[Eriadu Authority]] [[Battle of Sullust (Delvardus campaign)|attacked]] Sullust in [[5 ABY/Legends|5.5 ABY]], [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] Sovv led elements of the [[New Republic First Fleet]] to defend his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] and badly defeated the [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]]. After his victory at Sullust, [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Firmus Nantz]] dispatched a task force along the [[Rimma Trade Route/Legends|Rimma Trade Route]] to support Sovv and the Sullustans. Delvardus then lost a [[Battle of Sanrafsix (Galactic Civil War)|series]] [[Battle of Kabal (Galactic Civil War)|of]] [[Liberation of Clak'dor VII|battles]] [[Battle of Sluis Van (Delvardus campaign)|along]] his border at [[Sanrafsix/Legends|Sanrafsix]], [[Kabal/Legends|Kabal]], [[Clak'dor VII/Legends|Clak'dor]], and [[Sluis Van/Legends|Sluis Van]], which caused him to abandon his holdings and retreat to the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]].''[[The Essential Atlas]]''''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' ===Supreme Commander=== {{Quote|Nevertheless, we're exploding options for relocating key government and military personal to the Koornacht Cluster, or should worse come to worst, the Empress Teta system in the Deep Core|Sien Sovv proposal to relocate key New Republic personal to the Deep Core|''[[The New Jedi Order: Agents of Chaos II: Jedi Eclipse]]''}} Following the election of [[Borsk Fey'lya]] as [[Chief of State (New Republic)/Legends|Chief of State]], [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] Sovv was appointed [[Supreme Commander (Alliance)/Legends|Supreme Commander]] as one of Fey'lya's first acts of office, replacing the retiring Admiral [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Ackbar]]. Some believed that Sovv was chosen by Chief of State Fey'lya because he would be easy to control, though most accepted that the Admiral was a perfect caretaker of the peace time military. Realizing the value of counsel from his retired superior, Sovv ensured that Ackbar received a number of important analyses, keeping him informed on the state of the Defense Force. He also used his new rank to appoint his own Sullustan favorites (including [[Yeel]], [[Godt]], [[Muun (individual)|Muun]], [[Rabb]], and a dozen more) to exalted positions within the New Republic military.''[[The New Jedi Order: Star by Star]]'' ===Yuuzhan Vong invasion=== {{Quote|The moment is upon us, my friends. Please switch to your assigned battle channel now, and may the Force be with you.|Sien Sovv, addressing the New Republic Navy during the Battle of Coruscant|}} When the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] invaded [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], Admiral Sovv was suddenly faced with the prospect of fighting a wide scale war. The political turmoil in the New Republic government affected his ability to run the Defense Force. As the invaders pressed deeper into known space, Sovv struggled to formulate a plan to turn the tide of the war. His brainchild, using [[Centerpoint Station]] to trap the Yuuzhan Vong in the Corellian system, seriously backfired. The Yuuzhan Vong attacked Fondor instead, which resulted in the loss of nearly three-quarters of the Hapan Fleet. Sovv barely survived a Senate [[Vote of No Confidence/Legends|Vote of No Confidence]] in his leadership, which further eroded his confidence in leading the New Republic's forces. At the [[Battle of the Black Bantha]], Sovv actually commanded a fleet of New Republic warships in person, crippling a portion of the Vong fleet that was staging for an upcoming attack. Despite this major victory, the Yuuzhan Vong launched a massive armada under [[Tsavong Lah]], intent on capturing [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. Sovv's worst moment came while he was commanding the [[Battle of Coruscant (Yuuzhan Vong War)|defense of Coruscant]] against the Yuuzhan Vong in [[27 ABY/Legends|27 ABY]]. While in command of the Coruscant defense fleet, consisting of half the [[New Republic Defense Fleet/Legends|New Republic Defense Fleet]], he refused to open fire on the screen of refugee ships the Yuuzhan Vong were using to cover their advance, despite being expressly ordered to do so by Chief of State [[Borsk Fey'lya]]. He was then relieved of command, but Fey'lya had no means to enforce this, as Sovv retained control of the fleet. Elements of his fleet, led by [[Garm Bel Iblis/Legends|Garm Bel Iblis]], mutinied and obeyed Fey'lya's order, but could not turn the tide alone. The battle turned into a rout, while some Senators hijacked elements of the fleet for personal defense. As New Republic forces retreated towards [[Dac/Legends|Dac]], a despondent Admiral Sovv looked for someone to accept his resignation. ===Turning point=== After [[Cal Omas]]'s election as Chief of State, Sovv offered his resignation mere minutes after Omas was sworn in. Yet, the Sullustan admiral would soon find the confidence he needed to continue the war. Retired admiral Ackbar began advising the new government in a plan to turn the tide of the war. Sovv immediately offered to resign in favor of Ackbar, but the aging admiral refused. Instead of taking the reigns of the Defense Force, Ackbar performed a consultative role. While Sovv did not realize it at the time, Ackbar would restore his confidence. Sovv worked closely with Ackbar in readying the Defense Force for the Battle of Ebaq. Sovv oversaw the battle from a war room on Dac, leaving field command to Bothan admiral [[Traest Kre'fey]]. The battle was a stunning success, turning the tide of the war in favor of the New Republic. As the government transitioned into the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]], Sovv continued as the Supreme Commander. In the following months, Admiral Sovv showed new confidence, both in himself and his forces. His time in command saw the liberation of [[Fondor/Legends|Fondor]] and [[Yag'Dhul/Legends|Yag'Dhul]], though [[Second Battle of Bilbringi|Operation Trinity]] turned out a failure after the crippling of the [[HoloNet/Legends|HoloNet]]. Sien Sovv finally resigned after the [[Battle of Yuuzhan'tar]], leaving the command of the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]] military to [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] Admiral [[Traest Kre'fey]]. However, he was shortly reinstated due to Kre'fey's genocidal [[ar'krai]] leanings, and remained in the position over the next five years. He was lauded as being a capable administrator and peace-time commander - not a man of winning huge battles, but of defeating pirate groups and maintaining the peace. ===Swarm War=== After several years of commanding the [[Galactic Alliance Defense Force]] during the reconstruction of the galaxy, Sovv was killed in an accident when his personal transport was hit by another ship whose [[Vratix/Legends|Vratix]] pilot was flying under the influence of [[Membrosia/Legends|black membrosia]]. Evidence from an investigation by [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Corran Horn/Legends|Corran Horn]] pointed to sabotage. As membrosia was a drink used by the [[Gorog|Dark Nest]] [[Killik/Legends|Killiks]], many in the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]], including Chief Omas, believed the accident was arranged by the Dark Nest.''[[Dark Nest II: The Unseen Queen]]'' ===Legacy=== Whatever his own personal doubts about his abilities, the Sullustans held Sovv in the same degree of awe that the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] held for Ackbar and the [[Bothan/Legends|Bothans]] for Fey'lya and Kre'fey. He was buried on Sullust in one of the most impressive funerals the planet had ever seen, with numerous dignitaries from across the Alliance attending. ==Appearances== *''[[The New Jedi Order: Agents of Chaos I: Hero's Trial]]'' {{1st}} *''[[The New Jedi Order: Agents of Chaos II: Jedi Eclipse]]'' *''[[The New Jedi Order: Balance Point]]'' *''[[The New Jedi Order: Star by Star]]'' *''[[The New Jedi Order: Enemy Lines I: Rebel Dream]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[The New Jedi Order: Destiny's Way]]'' *''[[The New Jedi Order: Ylesia]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[The New Jedi Order: Force Heretic I: Remnant]]'' *''[[The New Jedi Order: Force Heretic III: Reunion]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[The New Jedi Order: The Final Prophecy]]'' *''[[The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force]]'' *''[[Dark Nest II: The Unseen Queen]]'' {{Mo}}
